title: Subtract decimals visually
description: Use tenths and hundredths charts to subtract decimals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Subtract.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the difference.* 

$0.54-0.14=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
hints: To subtract $\blueE{0.54}-\maroonD{0.14}$, we can  cross $\maroonD{0.14}$ off of the grid that shows $\blueE{0.54}$ to show subtraction.

[[☃ image 1]]

When we subtract $\maroonD{0.14}$ from $\blueE{0.54}$, we get $\blueE{40}$ hundredths.

We can change $\blueE{40}$ hundredths into tenths.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{40\text { hundredths}}$ is equal to $\blueE{4\text { tenths}}$:

$\blueE{0.40}=\blueE{0.4}$

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equally-sized smaller squares to represent hundredths. 54 squares are shaded and 46 squares are not shaded.
A square divided into 100 equally-sized smaller squares to represent hundredths. 14 squares are shaded and 86 squares are not shaded.

$\blueE{6\text { tenths}}$ is equal to $\blueE{60\text { hundredths}}$:

$\blueE{0.6}=\blueE{0.60}$

[[☃ image 1]]

When we subtract $\maroonD{0.58}$ from $\blueE{0.60}$, we get $\blueE{2}$ hundredths.
